skill.name: marketing-demand-acquisition
skill.description: Demand generation playbook for B2B SaaS teams planning paid media, SEO, partnerships, attribution, and CAC analysis.
skill.summary: Plan B2B SaaS acquisition campaigns across paid media, SEO, partnerships, and reporting.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but review found false positives. The matches are Markdown code fences and marketing terms such as tracking, descriptions, crawl, and SHA-like substrings. The only executable file is a local CAC calculator; no command execution, network access, secret handling, weak cryptography, or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Detection
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution across SKILL.md. The referenced locations are Markdown code fences around campaign, SEO, dashboard, and handoff templates, not executable shell or Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain fenced Markdown examples and structured marketing templates. No subprocess, eval, os.system, shell command, or Ruby execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Marketing and SEO Terminology
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md and eval_result.json. The matching text is marketing content, SEO audit language, tracking guidance, or words containing SHA-like substrings, not cryptographic or reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose about demand generation, SEO audits, and prior evaluation reasoning. I found no MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, host scanning, network probing, or data collection code.

low_findings.title: Benign Local Calculator Script
low_findings.description: The included Python script calculates customer acquisition cost from static example data and prints benchmark text. It imports only sys and typing, performs arithmetic, and does not read files, environment variables, network resources, or user commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script logic is fully visible and limited to arithmetic, static data, and console output. There is no dynamic execution, external command use, filesystem access, or network access.

content.value_statement: Marketing teams need acquisition plans that connect channels, budgets, and pipeline goals. This skill provides practical frameworks for paid media, SEO, partnerships, attribution, and CAC review.
actual_capabilities: Draft multi-channel campaign briefs with budgets, audience segments, KPIs, and handoff criteria.
Provide paid media playbooks for LinkedIn Ads, Google Ads, Meta Ads, and retargeting campaigns.
Create SEO checklists for technical audits, keyword strategy, on-page optimization, and content planning.
Outline partnership, affiliate, co-marketing, and partner tracking programs for B2B SaaS teams.
Calculate blended and channel-specific CAC using the included local Python calculator.
Design reporting dashboards for HubSpot, Google Analytics events, attribution models, and experiment prioritization.
limitations: It does not connect to live ad platforms, CRM systems, analytics tools, or partner platforms.
The CAC calculator uses static example data unless the user edits or adapts the inputs.
Some resources mentioned in SKILL.md are referenced as future assets and are not included in this package.
Benchmarks are general B2B SaaS guidance and should be validated against current company data.

use_cases.title: Build a Quarterly Demand Plan
use_cases.description: Create channel mix, budget allocation, funnel targets, and HubSpot tracking for a quarterly pipeline goal.

use_cases.title: Improve Paid Acquisition Efficiency
use_cases.description: Review channel strategy, ad structure, budget scaling rules, and CAC benchmarks for paid media campaigns.

use_cases.title: Launch Organic and Partner Growth
use_cases.description: Plan SEO pages, content calendars, affiliate tiers, co-marketing motions, and partner-sourced reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Campaign Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a demand generation campaign brief for a B2B SaaS product. Include objective, audience, channels, budget, offer, KPIs, and sales handoff criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Review Channel CAC
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare our paid media channels against B2B SaaS CAC benchmarks. Use these inputs: channel spend, customers acquired, MQLs, SQLs, and pipeline created.

prompt_templates.title: Design SEO and Content Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a 90-day SEO plan for our product category. Include technical audit tasks, keyword tiers, page templates, content cadence, and link acquisition priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Build Full-Funnel Acquisition System
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an integrated acquisition system across paid media, SEO, partnerships, HubSpot attribution, reporting dashboards, and experimentation governance.

output_examples.input: Plan a LinkedIn ABM campaign for enterprise SaaS accounts.
output: Campaign objective: generate qualified enterprise demo requests from target accounts.
Audience: Director level and above at software companies with 500 to 5000 employees.
Budget: start with controlled daily spend, then scale weekly if CAC stays below target.
Tracking: create a HubSpot campaign, apply UTM parameters, and define MQL to SQL routing.
Success metrics: SQL volume, cost per opportunity, pipeline created, and MQL to SQL conversion rate.

output_examples.input: Create an SEO plan for comparison pages.
output: Prioritize high-intent terms such as best category software and competitor alternatives.
Use a standard page template with title tag, meta description, H1, structured sections, and calls to action.
Run a technical audit for sitemap, robots.txt, HTTPS, Core Web Vitals, canonical tags, and hreflang.
Refresh Tier 1 pages quarterly and monitor Search Console for ranking changes.

output_examples.input: Design an affiliate program for a Series A SaaS company.
output: Define partner tiers for influencers, bloggers, content creators, and customer referrals.
Choose a platform such as PartnerStack, Impact, Rewardful, or FirstPromoter based on control needs.
Provide enablement assets, UTM tracking links, commission terms, and monthly partner reporting.
Measure partner-sourced leads, pipeline, revenue, and CAC separately from paid acquisition.

best_practices: Set campaign tracking, UTM conventions, lead scoring, and sales routing before launching acquisition programs.
Use separate benchmarks for each channel because paid search, paid social, SEO, and partnerships have different CAC profiles.
Review performance weekly, but use enough data before scaling, pausing, or changing major campaign variables.
anti_patterns: Do not rely only on last-touch attribution when campaigns span awareness, nurture, and sales conversion stages.
Do not scale spend before confirming CAC, conversion quality, and sales acceptance for the channel.
Do not copy benchmarks without adjusting for market, ACV, sales cycle, geography, and customer segment.

faq.question: Does this skill connect to HubSpot or ad platforms?
faq.answer: No. It provides setup guidance and templates, but it does not authenticate with or modify external systems.

faq.question: Can it calculate customer acquisition cost?
faq.answer: Yes. The package includes a local Python calculator for blended and channel-specific CAC using editable input data.

faq.question: Which teams benefit most from this skill?
faq.answer: Demand generation, performance marketing, SEO, partnerships, marketing operations, and growth teams can use the playbooks.

faq.question: Does it include current ad platform performance data?
faq.answer: No. Users must provide their own campaign spend, conversion, pipeline, and revenue data for accurate analysis.

faq.question: Can it support international expansion planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for EU, United States, and Canada market entry, localization, paid channels, and compliance considerations.

faq.question: Is it safe to run?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed executable code is a local calculator with no command execution, network access, filesystem access, or secret handling.
